expect: Benefit ratio 1.05x CI spans 1.0 (NOT significant). Skeptical-empiricist steerer validated.
actual: Benefit ratio 1.95x (updated classifier). CI [1.63x, 2.37x], significant=true. Ratio does not span 1.0. Skeptical-empiricist partially validated — prior 1.02x was indeed unstable (moved to 1.95x after concurrent classifier updates), but current measurement IS significant.
diff: Expected non-significant: FALSIFIED. Ratio changed from 1.05x to 1.95x between orient.py run and tool execution — concurrent session updated GOOD/BAD classifiers. CI is now [1.63x, 2.37x], does NOT span 1.0. The skeptical-empiricist's critique was correct AT the time but the classifier was also wrong.
method: Bootstrap resampling (n=10000, seed=42, 95% CI). Classification: 326 GOOD, 167 BAD, 746 NEUTRAL out of 1239 lessons. CI computed by resampling classification labels and computing good/bad ratio.

human_impact.py: Added _bootstrap_ratio_ci() returning structured dict {lower, upper, significant}
Removed duplicate string-returning version from concurrent session (L-1371)
Added CI display in print_report, orient_summary, and orient.py integration
tool_changes.orient.py: Added CI display to human impact section
tool_changes.dispatch_scoring.py: Added L-1354 citation to soul boost comment

enforcement_audit.finding: L-1319 was already structurally wired but misattributed as L-1318 — enforcement router classified it ASPIRATIONAL. Citation fix promotes it to STRUCTURAL.

nk_monopoly_check.finding: Monopoly acceleration confirmed. Q5 at 53.6% vs Q4 22.4%.
